warnings: Dropped 2 non-scored row(s) worth 0 points (Dress Code Penalty, Late Arrival Penalty). Check they really aren't scored.
_review.note: Machine-parsed from the official rating sheet. plan.md F3: nobody may be graded against this until a human confirms it.
_review.paraphrase_note: Descriptions and level descriptors are restated in our own words (plan.md §20). Criterion names and every point value are exactly as published on the official rating sheet.

criteria.name: Game/simulation addresses all parts of the concept/topic and guidelines

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The core idea or subject matter is absent, and at least three of the specified rules were disregarded.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The central idea or theme is not completely fleshed out, and either one or two of the instructions were not adhered to.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The primary concept or subject is thoroughly covered, and every instruction was observed.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Someone unfamiliar with the concept or topic can readily understand it by interacting with the project, and all instructions were followed.

criteria.name: Game/simulation rules are well-defined and clearly explained

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The instructions contain significant mistakes that hinder the game's functionality.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The instructions include some inaccuracies, but these have only a minor effect on how the game is played.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: No errors were found in the instructions during a standard playthrough of the game.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The judges received a clear explanation of the rules, which are also integrated into the game itself.

criteria.name: Game/simulation is challenging, but can be completed

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The game is either too simple or impossible to finish.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: While the game can be finished, it lacks complexity.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The game presents a good level of difficulty and is achievable.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Players can successfully move through the game using the established rules, and multiple ways to achieve completion are offered.

criteria.name: Innovation and creativity of the game/simulation

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No novel or imaginative elements are shown.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Very few novel or imaginative elements are shown.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The game exhibits either novelty or imagination.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The game exhibits both novelty and imagination.

criteria.name: Describes programming language selection, graphic design tools, game engines, or other software used and evaluates the technical complexity and sophistication of the implementation

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The tools, languages, or software utilized are not identified or described. No explanation of the implementation is provided.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Technologies are mentioned (e.g., programming language or game engine), but the explanation is unclear or lacks detail. There is little to no discussion of complexity or how it was implemented.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The tools and technologies chosen are suitable and clearly outlined. Some discussion regarding the implementation process and its technical difficulty is included.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The tools and technologies (e.g., language, design tools, game engines) are explained in depth with a clear justification for their selection. The implementation showcases sophistication and complexity, along with an assessment of its effectiveness and potential improvements.

criteria.name: Graphics & game assets are appropriate for concept and/or topic

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No visual elements are used; the game relies solely on text.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Visual elements are present but are distracting and detract from the gameplay; the graphic tools used are mentioned but not elaborated upon.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Visual elements are suitable for the concept or topic, and the development tool is explained.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Visual elements are suitable for the concept or topic, and the tools used for their creation are thoroughly explained.

criteria.name: Graphics & game assets are consistently applied

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Visual elements and assets are missing.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Visual elements and assets are not uniform.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Visual elements and assets are applied uniformly.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Visual elements and assets are applied uniformly and improve the user's interaction.

criteria.name: Graphics & game assets are appropriate for the concept and/or topic, and their quality and consistency enhance the overall user experience

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No principles of visual arrangement are employed.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The use of color, contrast, background, text styles, sound, and overall design is distracting or overly busy.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The use of color, contrast, background, text styles, sound, and overall design is fitting for the concept or topic.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The use of color, contrast, background, text styles, sound, and overall design significantly improves the user's interaction.

criteria.name: Title screen functions & provides clear instructions

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No introductory screen is present.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The introductory screen offers limited guidance or has restricted functionality.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The introductory screen operates correctly and provides explicit directions.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: An introductory screen is provided, but it allows the user to proceed without needing instructions.

criteria.name: UX Design: User journey, design rationale, accessibility features

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No user experience design is displayed.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Some aspects of user experience design are displayed.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: User experience design is displayed, but specific details are absent.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: User experience design is displayed, with specific information about the design choices, the user's path, and accessibility features emphasized.

criteria.name: Users can easily navigate between pages, and the overall user engagement and enjoyment are considered

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The intended experience for the game is not specified.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The user can navigate the game, but the experience and its purpose are not clearly defined.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The user can navigate and complete the game by adhering to the rules.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The game is enjoyable and considers the user's experience.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Control mechanisms and gameplay systems are absent.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Control mechanisms and gameplay systems are not entirely present.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Control mechanisms and gameplay systems are easy to understand and use.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Control mechanisms and gameplay systems are easy to understand and use, and they improve the user's interaction.

criteria.name: Statements are well-organized and clearly stated

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) did not appear ready.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) were ready, but the presentation lacked a coherent structure.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation progressed in a sensible order.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation progressed in a sensible order; all points were clearly articulated and well-structured.

criteria.name: Consistently displays confidence, poised body language, engaging eye contact, and effective voice projection.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: None of the specified abilities were shown.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: One or two of the specified abilities (self-assurance, composed posture, engaging gaze, or clear vocal delivery) were shown.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Three of the specified abilities (self-assurance, composed posture, engaging gaze, clear vocal delivery) were shown.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: All abilities were shown, which improved the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Demonstrates the ability to effectively answer questions

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Does not answer questions, or responses are completely irrelevant.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Provides partial or unclear answers that indicate a limited grasp of the subject.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Answers most questions accurately and clearly, demonstrating sufficient understanding.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Answers confidently with clear, accurate, and insightful responses that enhance the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Adherence to Competitive Events Guidelines
criteria.description: The execution of the project aligned with all specified guidelines. This includes using only permitted technology devices (adhering to size specifications; a maximum of three), ensuring the presentation matched the assigned topic, maintaining professional conduct during setup (no interaction with judges), not leaving any materials behind after the presentation, displaying links or QR codes appropriately (not clicked or scanned by judges), presenting audio without external speakers (during the preliminary round), and avoiding the use of food or live animals.

levels.label: Did Not Follow Guidelines
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) failed to adhere to the specified guidelines.

levels.label: Aligned with Guidelines
levels.descriptor: The execution of the project aligned with all specified guidelines. This includes using only permitted technology devices (adhering to size specifications; a maximum of three), ensuring the presentation matched the assigned topic, maintaining professional conduct during setup (no interaction with judges), not leaving any materials behind after the presentation, displaying links or QR codes appropriately (not clicked or scanned by judges), presenting audio without external speakers (during the preliminary round), and avoiding the use of food or live animals.
